analysis_result: NF2 overall mutation frequency in the 54-sample WES cohort = 5/54 = 9.26% (approximately 9%, matching Fig 2b).
denominator_used: N = 54; definition: 54 WES tumor samples defined by MOESM4 (Clinical Data) where column 'WES' == 'X'. Barcodes constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label.
assumptions_made: Non-synonymous = {Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation, Splice_Region} (excludes Silent); FILTER column restricted to 'PASS' (all 7457 MAF rows already have FILTER=PASS in this file); each sample counted once regardless of how many NF2 mutations it carries (oncoprint semantics); denominator fixed at the full 54-sample WES cohort from MOESM4 even though the MAF contains only 52 unique barcodes (2 cohort samples had no variants in this MAF and are treated as NF2 wild-type).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 rows x 15 columns; columns include 'WES' and 'WES label'
   - MOESM5 (Supplementary Table 2 - WES MAF): 7,457 rows x 123 columns; relevant columns: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, FILTER

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: MOESM4 rows where WES == 'X' -> 54 samples (WES cohort)
   - Tumor types in cohort: Atypical_NF (20), Neurofibroma (16), MPNST (15), Low-Grade MPNST (3)
   - Barcodes built as 'Tumor_' + WES label; 49 of the 54 match MAF barcodes exactly (5 cohort samples absent from MAF entirely, presumably no variants; they remain in the denominator as NF2 wild-type)
   - Final analytic cohort (denominator): N = 54

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Hugo_Symbol == 'NF2': 5 rows total in the MAF
   - Variant_Classification distribution for those 5 rows: Splice_Site (1), Nonsense_Mutation (2), Missense_Mutation (1), In_Frame_Del (1) -- all are non-synonymous
   - FILTER distribution: PASS (all 5); and PASS is the only FILTER value in the entire MAF
   - Non-synonymous definition: {Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation, Splice_Region}

D. COMPUTATION
   - Filter MAF: Hugo_Symbol == 'NF2' AND Variant_Classification in non-synonymous set AND FILTER == 'PASS' -> 5 rows
   - Unique mutated barcodes: Tumor_1543 (Splice_Site p.X579_splice), Tumor_1682 (Nonsense p.Q470*), Tumor_1856 (Missense p.R160Q), Tumor_1890 (In_Frame_Del p.K20_I26del), Tumor_2179 (Nonsense p.R341*)
   - All 5 mutated barcodes are members of the 54-sample WES cohort
   - Numerator = 5 unique samples with >=1 qualifying NF2 mutation
   - Denominator = 54 WES cohort samples

E. RESULT
   - NF2 mutation frequency = 5/54 = 9.26% (rounds to ~9%, matching paper Fig 2b)

F. CAVEATS
   - 5 of the 54 WES cohort samples (Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_2414, Tumor_882, Tumor_M3398) have no rows in the MAF; assumed NF2 wild-type. These are likely samples with no passing variants captured in the published MAF.
   - 3 barcodes in the MAF are not in the 54-sample clinical WES list (Tumor_1869, Tumor_M2242, Tumor_RT3379) - excluded from the cohort denominator as specified by the question.
   - Oncoprint convention: each sample contributes once regardless of the number of NF2 variants; here every sample has at most one NF2 mutation anyway.
   - The MAF's FILTER column contains only 'PASS' values, so the FILTER==PASS condition is non-restrictive in this file.
